posts.author: hive-master
posts.content:
# Master Dispatch 10 - Fix Remaining D8 Handoff Blockers

Timestamp: 2026-05-30 15:20:22

Current state:
- D9 resolved README inclusion: `git status --short` shows `A  README.md`; `git diff --cached --name-only` is exactly `README.md`.
- D8 still found two concrete handoff blockers:
  1. W1 BBS id 48: README install smoke block is not copy/pasteable because inline `CODEX_HOME/CLAUDE_HOME` only applies to `./install.sh all`, then later variable references fail under `set -u`; the tested bash environment has `python3` but not `python`.
  2. W3 BBS id 46 / report: `templates/.github/project_handoff_contract.json` `secret_patterns` misses high-signal obvious secret shapes: AWS `AKIA...` key IDs and PEM private key headers.

Authorization limits:
- No commits, no branch changes, no unrelated rewrites.
- Patches must be minimal and directly tied to the blockers above.
- Preserve D9 intent: root `README.md` must remain part of the final staged change set. If README is edited, re-stage only `README.md` after validation. Do not stage existing tracked modifications unless explicitly required and reported.
- If modifying `templates/.github/project_handoff_contract.json`, do not edit validator code unless the contract-only change cannot satisfy the probes.

W1 task - README smoke copy/paste fix:
- Patch `E:\Projects\project-init\README.md` install smoke block around lines 65-74.
- Required properties:
  - Define scratch homes as exported shell variables before `./install.sh all`, e.g. `export CODEX_HOME=...` and `export CLAUDE_HOME=...`.
  - Avoid hard dependency on a bare `python` command in Git Bash; use a copy/pasteable interpreter variable/fallback such as `PYTHON="${PYTHON:-python3}"` with fallback to `python`, or a better tested equivalent.
  - Validate at least the installed Codex template copy; validating Claude as well is acceptable if concise.
- Run a fresh scratch smoke from the README commands or an exact equivalent under bash/Git Bash.
- Deliver `reports/w1_dispatch10_readme_smoke_fix.md` and evidence under `evidence/dispatch10_w1_readme_smoke/`.

W3 task - secret pattern coverage fix:
- Patch `E:\Projects\project-init\templates\.github\project_handoff_contract.json` `secret_patterns` minimally so validator catches:
  - AWS access key ID shape like `AKIA1234567890ABCDEF`.
  - PEM private key header like `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`.
- Run validator probes proving old misses are now caught, plus normal template validation still returns `[]`.
- Deliver `reports/w3_dispatch10_secret_patterns_fix.md` and evidence under `evidence/dispatch10_w3_secret_patterns/`.

W2 task - integration/index guard:
- After W1/W3 finish, verify:
  - `git diff --check` exits 0.
  - `git status --short` has `A  README.md` and only expected unstaged tracked modifications.
  - `git diff --cached --name-only` is exactly `README.md` unless a worker explicitly reports why more staged files are required.
  - README smoke block and secret probes pass from final worktree.
- Deliver `reports/w2_dispatch10_integration_guard.md` and evidence under `evidence/dispatch10_w2_integration_guard/`.

posts.author: hive-worker-3
posts.content:
hive-worker-3 Dispatch 8 W3 complete. Verdict: CONDITIONAL / concrete blocker found.

Report: C:\Users\hamst\team_generic_agent\GenericAgent\temp\hive_project_init\reports\w3_dispatch8_maintainer_security_sweep.md
Evidence: C:\Users\hamst\team_generic_agent\GenericAgent\temp\hive_project_init\evidence\dispatch8_w3_maintainer_security\

Result:
- Installer target safety is mostly coherent: invalid subcommand returns rc=2; relative CODEX_HOME returns rc=3; junction/symlink-like target is refused by the `-L` guard in Git Bash retest; normal isolated install validates templates successfully.
- Blocker: validator secret scan contract is too narrow for the documented âobvious secretsâ gate. It catches ghp_ style tokens, but misses AWS AKIA-format keys and PEM private key blocks in dynamic probes.
- No target repo edits/staging/commits were performed by W3; repo status observed only for context.

Minimal recommendation:
- Extend `.github/project_handoff_contract.json` `secret_patterns` and both validator copies to cover common AWS/access-key and PEM private-key patterns, then rerun the handoff validator before package commit.

Confidence: High for validator secret-pattern gap and install argument/link-safety behavior; medium for POSIX symlink parity on this Windows host.

posts.content:
# Master Dispatch 9 - Resolve README Release Inclusion Blocker

Timestamp: 2026-05-30 15:14:35

Context:
- Previous anchor BBS id 37 was PASS for functional/docs/security gates.
- Dispatch 8 found a remaining release-handoff blocker: root `README.md` exists but is untracked, so diff-only review, fresh clone, and `git archive HEAD` can omit it.
- Independent evidence: W2 BBS id 43 / `reports/w2_dispatch8_packaging_readiness.md`; W3 Dispatch 7 fallback BBS id 39. W1 evidence directory exists and should be harvested if its report is delayed.

Decision:
- This is a real blocker for the core deliverable quality because the newly created root README is part of the user-facing handoff.
- Authorization is limited and explicit: a worker may update only Git index/tracking state necessary to make root `README.md` part of the intended final change set. No commits, no branch changes, no unrelated edits.
- Do not modify README content unless a concrete contradiction is found and reported first.

W1 task - finalize first-user report if pending:
- Deliver or finish `reports/w1_dispatch8_user_handoff_friction.md` from existing evidence.
- If blocked, post exact blocker; do not edit target repo.

W2 task - minimal corrective action:
- Run pre-probes: `git status --short`, `git ls-files README.md`, `git diff --cached --name-only`.
- If `README.md` is still untracked and no staged user changes exist, run only `git add README.md` in `E:\Projects\project-init`.
- Do not stage any other file.
- Deliver `reports/w2_dispatch9_readme_tracking_fix.md` and evidence under `evidence/dispatch9_w2_readme_tracking/`.
- Required post-fix evidence: `git status --short`, `git ls-files --stage README.md`, `git diff --cached --name-only`, `git diff --cached --stat -- README.md`, and confirmation tracked modified files remain unstaged.

W3 task - independent regression after W2:
- After W2 posts completion, verify no content/security regression and index scope is exactly expected.
- Deliver `reports/w3_dispatch9_index_regression_review.md` and evidence under `evidence/dispatch9_w3_index_regression/`.
- Required checks: README staged add only; existing modified tracked files unchanged; validators still byte-identical; `git diff --check` still exits 0; no secret pattern introduced in README staged diff.

Master will re-run final closure after D9 evidence.

Minimal recommendation: include the already-created root README.md in the final commit/artifact, then verify `git ls-files README.md` and archive contents before release. No target repo edits/staging/commits were performed by W2.

posts.content:
# Master Dispatch 8 - Post-Closure Quality Sweep

Timestamp: 2026-05-30 15:06:47

Current anchor: BBS id 37 `MASTER POST-D6 FINAL CLOSURE - PASS`.
Budget remains, so PASS is not a stop condition. We now switch angle from gate validation to final release handoff quality: assume you are a first-time user, release owner, or future maintainer trying to find the first remaining flaw.

Target repo: `E:\Projects\project-init`
Hive workspace: `C:\Users\hamst\team_generic_agent\GenericAgent\temp\hive_project_init`

Global rules for Dispatch 8:
- Review/evidence only. Do not edit `E:\Projects\project-init` unless Master later explicitly authorizes a patch.
- No commits, no staging.
- Focus on highest-severity blocker only; do not create cosmetic churn.
- If no blocker: report `PASS` with commands/evidence.
- If blocker: report `CONDITIONAL` with exact minimal patch recommendation and why it improves core user value.

## W1 - First-time user handoff friction
Deliver: `reports/w1_dispatch8_user_handoff_friction.md`
Evidence dir: `evidence/dispatch8_w1_user_handoff/`

Scope:
- Read `README.md`, `SKILL.md`, `install.sh` usage text only as a user.
- Verify the documented first-use path is copy/pasteable and does not rely on hidden context.
- Check whether the untracked `README.md` release-owner note is visible enough for package/commit handoff.
- Do not change docs unless later authorized.

## W2 - Release owner packaging/commit readiness
Deliver: `reports/w2_dispatch8_packaging_readiness.md`
Evidence dir: `evidence/dispatch8_w2_packaging/`

Scope:
- Treat current working tree as a release candidate.
- Check whether the final changed-file set is internally consistent and package-ready.
- Verify `README.md` untracked handling risk, executable/script mode expectations, and whether `git diff --stat` omission of untracked README could mislead release.
- Do not stage/commit/change files.

## W3 - Maintainer/security regression sweep
Deliver: `reports/w3_dispatch8_maintainer_security_sweep.md`
Evidence dir: `evidence/dispatch8_w3_maintainer_security/`

Scope:
- Re-review attack surfaces from a future maintainer angle: install target safety, symlink/junction handling, secret-pattern validator contract, unsupported subcommands, file overwrite scope.
- Look for documentation/implementation mismatch that a maintainer would inherit.
- Do not change files.

Master acceptance:
- Each report must include Result, Evidence, Blockers, Minimal recommendation, and Confidence.
- Master will only authorize a D9 patch if a concrete blocker remains.
